---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)


```

```{css}

```

## My report tracks the altitude, airline and destination/origin of flights over auckland.

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
library(tidyverse)

#reading in the data
csv_file <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vR0V68ZyxYi4ji1powjGlt6urn0_P5I7I5QNaugI_Hf54eUNXbSKr9sMYqD9omV6Lgn6tRaaFNL-Ff1/pub?output=csv"
logged_data <- read_csv(csv_file)

#renaming the data to a new data frame 
latest_data <- read_csv(csv_file) %>%
  rename(int_domestic = 2,
  altitude = 3,
  airline = 4)

#finding out what the minimum altitude is
min_alt <- min(latest_data$altitude)

#finding out what the maximum altitude is
max_alt <- max(latest_data$altitude)

#plotting the amount of international versus domestic flights
ggplot(data = latest_data) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = int_domestic))

#plotting what airline the fights were from
ggplot(data = latest_data) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = airline))

paste("The minimum altitude is", min_alt, "feet.")
paste("The maximum altitude is", max_alt, "feet.")



pets <- c("cat", "dog", "turtle", "rabbit")
best_pet <- pets[1]

```

The best pet to have is a `r best_pet` of course!

![](https://c.tenor.com/-qBsG1HwR4oAAAAC/tenor.gif)
